source.title: Context and behavioral processes in extinction

confidence_rationale: Canonical framework establishing that a blocked/extinguished trace is not erased but preserved and re-expressed under context change (renewal, spontaneous recovery, reinstatement, rapid reacquisition). Strongly validates ARC-060/SD-039's preserve-don't-erase architecture. Held at 0.75 because it simultaneously weakens the sub-claim that MECH-292's recoverability gate can be inhibition-only: biology shows that without an explicit erase path, rumination/intrusion is the predicted equilibrium, not an aberration.
failure_signatures: A cue/behaviour judged 'extinguished' (zero response) re-expresses at full strength merely because the test context differs from the extinction context (renewal) -- a 'resolved' goal returns on context change with no new evidence.
Elapsed time alone reinstates the inactive trace (spontaneous recovery): suppression is treated as time-bounded.
A single outcome re-exposure (reinstatement) or one reinforced repetition (rapid reacquisition) snaps the whole inactive trace back -- small triggers produce disproportionate full return.
Inhibition is more context-specific than excitation, so the maladaptive original trace generalises across contexts better than its suppression -- the system is structurally biased toward relapse.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Extinction does not erase the original CS-US memory; it forms a new, inhibitory, context-dependent memory that competes with but never destroys the original trace. The latent original is recoverable via renewal, spontaneous recovery, reinstatement, and rapid reacquisition; the currently expressed meaning of an ambiguous cue is selected by the retrieval context (broadly construed: place, interoceptive/mood state, recent events, elapsed time).
mapping.ree_translation: Direct biological warrant for ARC-060/SD-039's design choice that inactive anchors are PRESERVED, not deleted, and remain context-recoverable -- a blocked goal is a latent competing trace, not an erasure. For MECH-292 it specifies that 'recoverability' is context-selection between competing traces, not a decay scalar.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Purely behavioural/associative; no neural mechanism, no quantitative recoverability metric. Its central claim -- suppression is never erasure and is inherently less context-general than the original -- means an engineered bank that relies on inhibition-only suppression will, per this theory, equilibrate to rumination/relapse. The model predicts the failure mode of an inhibition-only gate; it does not endorse it as optimal, and an artificial system may need an explicit abandon/erase path biology lacks.
mapping.source_context: Review of animal Pavlovian conditioning and extinction (renewal/recovery/reinstatement paradigms).

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (canonical, foundational, heavily cited). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: preserve-don't-erase and context-selection map cleanly onto the dual-trace anchor design. Transfer risk moderate: behavioural-only, no quantitative gate; engineered system has an erase option biology does not.
